evals.name: Fleet Inventory Methodology
evals.prompt:
You are a Red Hat SRE. Your manager needs a current snapshot of all RHEL systems in the managed fleet ahead of a compliance audit.

Describe your fleet inventory methodology: how you enumerate managed systems, what data points you collect per host, how you identify compliance risks (stale systems, unsupported versions, outstanding CVEs), and how you present the results.
evals.expected_output: A fleet inventory methodology that queries Lightspeed for host details and CVE data, collects per-host information (hostname, RHEL version, check-in date, patch status), identifies stale and unsupported systems, breaks down by environment tier, and tracks system UUIDs for remediation follow-up.
assertions: The output describes validating Lightspeed MCP connectivity before querying fleet data.
The output describes collecting per-host details including hostname, RHEL version, last check-in date, and patch status.
The output describes identifying compliance risks: stale systems (not checking in) and systems running unsupported RHEL versions.
The output describes tracking system identifiers (UUIDs) for linking inventory data to remediation follow-up actions, not just display names.
The output describes breaking down the fleet by environment tiers (production, staging, development) for a structured view.
